topic: AGENTS.md: The New File Every Repo Needs for AI Agent Governance
hook: There's a new file that controls how AI agents work in your repo. Here's how.
audience: Developers and team leads adopting AI coding tools
keyTakeaway: AGENTS.md lets you define rules and constraints that AI agents must follow in your repository, bringing governance to agentic development.
talkingPoints: Explain the new AGENTS.md convention (from OpenAI, adopted by GitHub)
Show how Copilot coding agent respects the nearest AGENTS.md file in directory tree
Demo creating an AGENTS.md with project-specific rules and constraints
Compare with copilot-instructions.md—when to use which file
Show real example: adding 'never modify files in /config' rule and testing it
trendContext: GitHub now supports AGENTS.md files for agent-specific instructions, with the nearest file taking precedence—a new standard that's not widely known yet among developers.
